title: Video by musicallyproper
description:
In 1982, Conrad SchnitzlerвҖ”once a member of Tangerine Dream and KlusterвҖ”dropped вҖҳThe Shark Eats Ice.вҖҷ A track so icy, mechanical, and satirical it feels like electroclash 20 years early.

Schnitzler came from BerlinвҖҷs art-school avant-garde, where Dada thrived on absurd, vivid phrases that stripped words of rational meaning. 

Born during WWI, Dada was an anti-art movement that embraced nonsense and provocation as a way to reject logic and convention. 

вҖҳThe Shark Eats IceвҖҷ feels like exactly thatвҖ”a machine-beat predator devouring something cold and empty, a surreal title that mirrors the trackвҖҷs satire of consumer electronics and the dehumanizing chill of machine culture.

(Visuals by the synth performance art pioneers, Tik and Tok)

#conradschnitzler #electro #electroclash #synthmusic #mimes

Intermittent hypoxia is something IвҖҷve been doing for 4+ years now. It has helped me clear my sinuses, overcome injuries and gain a better quality of life and well-being.

title: Post by uncover.quantum
description:
рҹҢҢ A RUSSIAN QUANTUM PHYSICIST DISCOVERED HOW TO SLIDE BETWEEN PARALLEL REALITIES рҹҢҢ
This is genuinely mind-bending. In 1993, Vadim Zeland quit his job after a profound insight: reality exists as infinite parallel tracks, like movie versions already filmed. And we can вҖңslideвҖқ between them by changing our inner state.
His counter-intuitive discovery:
The harder you want something, the further it gets pushed away. Treating goals like вҖңno big dealвҖқ makes them manifest faster than desperately wanting them ever could.
Why this happens:
When you assign high importance to something, you create вҖңexcess potentialвҖқ - energetic resistance that the universe balances by creating obstacles. The more you push, the more resistance you generate.
His 3-step no-big-deal method:
Step 1: Lower the importance
Say вҖңIt would be nice if this happened, but itвҖҷs no big deal if it doesnвҖҷt.вҖқ Feel the relief when you release the pressure.
Step 2: Act as if itвҖҷs already arranged
Move through your day assuming the universe has handled it. Take inspired action, but donвҖҷt force.
Step 3: Slide between realities
When obstacles appear, imagine stepping sideways into a parallel reality where they donвҖҷt exist.
Why this works:
Your emotional state broadcasts a frequency attracting matching circumstances. Desperation attracts more desperation. Calm confidence attracts opportunities.
The no-big-deal state is the highest manifestation frequency because it contains zero resistance.
What to watch for:
Synchronicities increase. Doors open easily. Helpful people appear. Obstacles dissolve. These are signs youвҖҷre sliding to better timelines.
The truth:
What you chase runs away. What you allow comes closer. Your desires exist in parallel realities right now - you just need to slide into the right track by changing your inner state.

Follow @uncover.quantum for reality-bending insights that challenge everything

#RealityTransurfing #QuantumPhysics #Manifestation #ParallelRealities #VadimZeland #ConsciousnessScience #QuantumMechanics #RealityShifting #MindScienceвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢвҖӢ

title: Video by jeetinsight
description:
Lanthimos refused to explain the ending, saying mystery was essential.

Dogtooth (2009), directed by Yorgos Lanthimos, is one of the most unsettling and unique films of modern European cinema. The story explores extreme family control, isolation, and the ways people can manipulate reality.

The filmвҖҷs intense scenes were filmed with practical effects, minimal music, and precise acting choices to maintain tension and unease. Actors were instructed to keep emotional reactions minimal, adding to the eerie, controlled atmosphere. Critics call it one of the most controversial and talked-about films in recent years, praised for its bold storytelling, strange world-building, and unforgettable visual style. 
.
.
.

title: Video by expansionwithdrjess
description:
Most people think manifestation is about what you want.

ItвҖҷs not. ItвҖҷs about who your nervous system thinks you are.

And that identity was formed long before you ever chose it. When you were little, your brain lived in a highly programmable state.

You absorbed tone, chaos, pressure, roles, expectationsвҖҰ
and your body built an identity around whatever felt safest.
The strong one.
The invisible one.
The caretaker.
The perfectionist.
The one who doesnвҖҷt need anything.
The one who earns love by overfunctioning.
That isnвҖҷt personality. ThatвҖҷs survival coding.

And hereвҖҷs the kicker:
Your nervous system will recreate ANY reality that matches that identity.

Not because youвҖҷre sabotaging yourself,
but because your body is loyal to whatever once kept you alive.

If you want a new reality, you donвҖҷt need more affirmations.

You need a new identity.

рҹ‘Ү Tell me in the comments:
Which survival identity do YOU resonate with?
The strong one, the pleaser, the perfectionist, the invisible one, or something else entirely?

вңЁ Follow for Part 3: Why Your Nervous System DoesnвҖҷt Believe Your Affirmations.

Have you heard of this silent epidemic? sharing my story because if someone would have shared this information with me a few years ago, I could have avoided being in a wheelchair and having to learn how to walk again.

these antibiotics are often handed out like candy with no regard for how serious side effects can be. the short list is they can attack your central nervous system, gut, brain, heart, tendons/nerves, eyes and kidneys. they cause mitochondrial damage and the FDA has said (for YEARS) that they should be reserved for life and death situations. often times doctors are completely unaware of these debilitating side effects as theyвҖҷre commonly prescribed for all sorts of minor issues like UTIs and ear infections. 

The main FQ Antibiotics to educate yourself on before itвҖҷs too late: 
ciprofloxacin (Cipro)
gemifloxacin (Factive)
levofloxacin (Levaquin)
moxifloxacin (Avelox)
ofloxacin (Floxin)

вһЎпёҸ FQ eyedrops and ear drops also can flox you рҹҳ®вҖҚрҹ’Ё these are also given out to pets. рҹ’Җ

FQs can also have a cumulative effect and even if youвҖҷve done вҖңfineвҖқ with it in the past, itвҖҷs like playing russian roulette вҖ” you can have a reaction years later and вҖңthe bombвҖқ can go off with sometimes only one pill. 

this time 3 years ago i was suffering with 70+ рҹҳірҹҳірҹҳі unexplainable symptoms and had to connect the dots on my own. I wasnвҖҷt sure if i would survive it. today i have less than 5 symptoms. рҹҳӯвқӨпёҸвҖҚрҹ©№

title: Video by movies_times_12
description:
Caligula (1979) is one of the most controversial films ever made, blending historical drama with shocking explicit content. Inspired by the real-life Roman emperor Caligula, the movie follows his rise to power and his descent into madness, cruelty, and extreme obsession.

The film depicts ancient Rome as a place of absolute power, corruption, violence, and unchecked desire. CaligulaвҖҷs rule is shown as brutal and chaotic, where human lives, loyalty, and morals mean nothing. As emperor, he indulges in sadistic pleasures, manipulation, and public humiliation, slowly destroying everyone around himвҖ”including himself.

What makes Caligula infamous is not just its story, but its explicit scenes, which caused massive controversy worldwide. Many versions of the film were banned, censored, or edited in several countries. Despite this, the movie gained cult status for its bold themes, disturbing imagery, and portrayal of how power without limits can turn a man into a monster.

Caligula is not an easy watch. ItвҖҷs disturbing, uncomfortable, and extremeвҖ”but for many, it remains a symbol of cinema without boundaries, showing the darkest side of human desire and authority.
. 
#Caligula1979 #ControversialCinema #CultFilm #RomanEmpire #DisturbingMovies #BannedMovies #DarkCinema #HistoricalDrama #ExtremeCinema #CultClassic

title: Video by cinesospeso
description:
Osgood Perkins has officially cemented his status as the architect of modern atmospheric horror with Keeper (2025). Collaborating again with cinematographer Jeremy Cox, Perkins utilizes suffocating negative space and deliberate, voyeuristic framing to transform a simple cabin retreat into a claustrophobic nightmare. The terror here is purely architectural; the camera lingers on empty corners and open doorways, forcing the audience to actively scan the frame for threats that may not even exist.
Tatiana Maslany delivers a career-defining performance of unraveling sanity, grounding the folk-horror elements in a terrifyingly tangible emotional reality. While the screenplay by Nick Lepard builds a foundation of relationship toxicity, it is PerkinsвҖҷ precise control over tone and sound designвҖ”specifically Edo Van BreemenвҖҷs subliminal scoreвҖ”that instills a physical sense of dread.This film proves that true horror lies not in the monster, but in the silence before it appears.

Follow @cinesospeso for cinema analysis.
.
.

#horrormovies #horrorfan #creepy

title: Video by explaining
description:
Follow (us) @Explaining to Learn something NEW every day рҹ§ рҹ’« 

Andy Serkis transformed movie history with his performance as Gollum in The Lord of the Rings. рҹ§ҷвҖҚвҷӮпёҸрҹҺӯ

Instead of just lending his voice, Serkis performed the character physically on set using motion capture, crawling, twisting, and acting alongside the cast so animators could capture every movement and emotion. His split personality, switching between Gollum and SmГ©agol, brought depth, tragedy, and realism to a fully digital character. рҹҺҘвңЁ

The role proved that motion capture could be true acting, not just animation, and it changed how films approach digital characters forever.

Let us know your thoughts below!

вҖў

#explore #storytelling #interesting #reels #learn

title: Post by sdcaw_spirituality
description:
In 1985, Dr. Bruce Lipton found something unsettling: your cells are not responding to realityвҖ”theyвҖҷre responding to what you think reality is. 

He called it the Perception Code. рҹ§ вңЁ

Bruce Lipton was a cell biologist. 

He studied stem cellsвҖ”same DNA, different environments, different outcomes. 

One dish created bone. 

One created muscle. 

One created fat. Genes werenвҖҷt in controlвҖ”the environment was. 

And what controlled the environment? 

Your perception. Your beliefs. 

Your cells respond to the world you perceive, not the world that exists. рҹҢҖ

Fear changes cell chemistry. 

Stress weakens immunity. 

Hope boosts regeneration. 

Thoughts arenвҖҷt mentalвҖ”theyвҖҷre molecular. 

Your perception writes instructions on your DNA. 

Most people live in a stress-based perception installed during childhood, keeping the body in survival mode. Healing, growth, creativity shut down. рҹ’«

The 3-step method: Interrupt the fear signal. 

Install a new perception (вҖңWhat if this works for me?вҖқ). 

Feel the future stateвҖ”emotion flips the genetic switch. 

Rewrite perception вҶ’ rewrite chemistry вҶ’ rewrite reality. рҹ”‘

Comment вҖңBLUEPRINTвҖқ for the complete system.

.

.

title: Post by thequantumbrief
description:
In 1977, inventor Itzhak Bentov met with intelligence officials to share a radical idea: The universe is a hologram within human awareness.

The CIA later produced a classified 29-page report titled Analysis and Assessment of Gateway Process. It concluded that human consciousness could move beyond physical space and time.

The Missing Page 25 Kept classified for decades, this page eventually revealed statements like вҖңTime is a construct of the conscious mindвҖқ and that вҖңThere is no past or future, only NOW.вҖқ This linked meditation, quantum theory, and brainwave synchronization into a practical framework.

The Method BentovвҖҷs approach involved entering the theta state, synchronizing both brain hemispheres using binaural beats, and applying focused intention to reshape memory and influence probability.

The Tragic End Bentov died in the 1979 Flight 191 crash while traveling to present his findings. He left behind a powerful legacy and one core message:

вҖңYou are not a body living in the universe. You are the universe experiencing itself through a body.вҖқ

title: Video by judgysociety
description:
рҹ§®

Kaprekar's constant is the number 6174, a four-digit number that emerges when you repeatedly apply a specific process: take any four-digit number (with at least two different digits), rearrange its digits to form the largest and smallest possible numbers, and subtract the smaller from the larger, always ensuring four digits (using leading zeros if needed). This "Kaprekar's routine" always converges to 6174 within seven iterations, after which the process yields 6174 indefinitely (e.g., 7641 - 1467 = 6174).

title: Video by buelistic
description:
You are not living in an objective world.
You are living inside your nervous system- regulated or unregulated

Neuroscience studies ave discovered that over 90% of sensory data is filtered out before it ever reaches your conscious awareness. What you call вҖңrealityвҖқ is just your brain predicting what matters most for its safety &survival(this defense is built from past experiences, emotional memories, and expectation)
Like I said, Two people can be in the same room, hear the same words, experience the same eventвҖҰ and process entirely different worlds.

Quantum physics shows us why this matters.
At the subatomic level, particles do not exist as fixed objects. They exist as probabilities of existence. They exist as potential waves/frequencies until measured. Observation-aka your focus-isnвҖҷt passive. It forces reality to select. Then It collapses all those different possibilities into one form.

вҡЎпёҸAgain! Remember Your thoughts are electrical signals traveling through neural networks.
рҹ§ІYour emotions are magnetic fields generated by the heart & body.Together, they form a signal and it trains what your brain notices, what your body prepares for, and as a result trains the world to show which probabilities to bring to the surface of your experience.
This is why repetition works.
This is why visualization works.
This is why beliefрҹ‘үрҹҸҪwhen paired with emotional certaintyрҹ‘ҲрҹҸҪchanges outcomes. Not because youвҖҷre вҖңpretending.вҖқBut because the nervous system cannot distinguish between an experience vividly imagined and one physically lived. The body calibrates first. Reality follows.

вҖңReality is merely an illusion, albeit a very persistent one.вҖқ
-AAlbert Einstein
The Quanta 2.0 breaks this down precisely, what the world is, how to regulate your state while experiencing it, building coherence, and intentionally raising the probability of the life you want to experience. And once you understand the system, you stop asking for permission, you start directing outcomes.

title: Video by antiagingmission
description:
This One Breathing Technique Increases Brain Blood Flow by 500%

Yes вҖ” 5x more blood to your brain and heart just by how you breathe.

HereвҖҷs how it works:
1. Take 30 deep, rhythmic breaths вҖ” in through the nose, out through the mouth.
2. After the final exhale, hold your breath for as long as you can (aim for ~90 seconds).
3. During this breath-hold, blood flow to the brain and heart increases up to fivefold.

title: Video by film.paranoia
description:
A Page of Madness (1926), directed by Teinosuke Kinugasa, is a landmark of Japanese experimental cinema and one of the most radical silent films ever made. Set almost entirely inside a mental asylum, the film follows a former sailor who takes a job as a janitor in order to stay close to his wife, a patient institutionalized after a psychological breakdown. Rather than telling its story through conventional narrative, the film immerses the viewer in a fractured, subjective world that mirrors the inner lives of its characters.

#JapaneseCinema #FilmHistory #SilentFilm
#Cinephile#APageOfMadness #ExperimentalFilm #AvantGardeCinema #CultCinema#FilmReel #ArthouseFilm #WorldCinema

title: Video by sheturnedsavage
description:
No one talks about this part.

When pain has been your shadow for years,
it stops feeling like something you carry вҖ”
and starts feeling like who you are.

YouвҖҷve built yourself around it.
Learned how to function with it.
Learned how to explain yourself through it.

The sadness became your language.
The anxiety became your rhythm.
The grief became your identity.

So when someone says, вҖңYouвҖҷre healing,вҖқ
instead of reliefвҖҰ thereвҖҷs fear.

Who am I without the ache?
Who am I without the story that shaped me?
Who am I without the battles that carved themselves into my bones?

Healing sounds beautiful in theory.
But in reality?
It feels like walking into a quiet room after years of chaos.

And the quiet is unfamiliar.
The quiet is uncomfortable.
The quiet asks you to meet yourself without the armor.

You are not strange for being scared of that.
You are not weak for hesitating at the edge of peace.

When pain has been your compass,
letting it go can feel like losing direction.

But hereвҖҷs the truth:

You are not your suffering.
You are not the worst thing that happened to you.
You are not the grief that shaped you.

You are the person who survived it.

And the version of you that exists beyond the pain?
She isnвҖҷt empty.
She isnвҖҷt lost.
She isnвҖҷt unrecognisable.

SheвҖҷs softer.
Stronger.
Quieter in the best way.

ItвҖҷs okay to be scared of meeting her.

Just donвҖҷt run from her.

рҹ•ҠпёҸ Follow @sheturnedsavage for grief quotes that hold your heart while you learn who you are outside the hurt.

рҹ’¬ If youвҖҷre struggling with your mental health and navigating healing feels overwhelming,
comment or DM вҖңworkbook.вҖқ
You donвҖҷt have to figure this out alone.

#HealingIsScary
#GriefJourney
#IdentityAfterLoss

title: Video by lawsofattraction2026
description:
In 1994, Jim Carrey was so broke he was sleeping in his car.

Auditions kept rejecting him.
Bills kept piling up.
Hope was running thin.

Most people wouldвҖҷve quit.

Instead, Jim did something strange.

Every night, he sat alone and pictured a different future.
Not desperately.
Not angrily.
Calmly.

He imagined himself acting in major films.
He imagined financial freedom.
He imagined success so clearly it felt real.

One day, with almost no money to his name, he wrote himself a check for $10 million and dated it five years into the future.

People laughed at that part.

But what they didnвҖҷt see was the shift happening inside him.

He stopped chasing.
He started aligning.

Years later, right on time, he received $10 million for his role in вҖңDumb and Dumber.вҖқ

That wasnвҖҷt luck.
That was a trained mind.

I think about this whenever I hear stories of people stuck in survival mode вҖ” like a cash-on-delivery rider delivering packages all day, touching money that never stays with him, believing wealth is only for others.

Until he changes one thing.

His focus.

A few quiet minutes each morning.
No phone.
No noise.

Just intention, gratitude, and belief.

Not to force moneyвҖҰ
but to become the person who attracts it.

Because money doesnвҖҷt respond to struggle.
It responds to clarity.

If youвҖҷre working hard but feel stuck, maybe your effort isnвҖҷt the problem.

Maybe your mindset is asking for an upgrade.

title: Video by stelle.world
description:
The phrase highest form of intelligence isn't IQ sets the tone for a thought-provoking discussion. According to neuroscientists, this intelligence is something entirely different, and it's rare to find people who possess it. The conversation revolves around metacognition, the ability to think about one's own thinking.

This video delves into the concept of metacognition, explaining that it's the ability to observe one's mind in real time, noticing thoughts, questioning reactions, and interrupting emotional reflexes. The conversation highlights the importance of updating beliefs instead of defending them, and how this process can lead to changes in the brain. The anterior prefrontal cortex, a region responsible for self-observation, activates when one observes their own thoughts. The video also displays on-screen text, highest update beliefs interrupting emotions that can edit its own, which reinforces the idea that metacognition allows for a deeper understanding of one's thoughts and emotions. By exploring this concept, the video provides insight into the workings of the human brain and the potential for personal growth.

The idea that metacognition can be developed and strengthened over time raises interesting questions about personal development and intelligence. As the conversation suggests, quietly outgrowing others around them, those who develop metacognition can experience significant personal growth. What does it mean to truly think about one's own thinking, and how can this ability be cultivated in daily life? The video invites viewers to consider the role of metacognition in their own lives, and to reflect on the potential benefits of developing this rare form of intelligence.

#Metacognition #psychology #explore

Follow @stelle.world for tech, science and everything in between. Even intelligence, in some form this is true, but in some form this is dubious.

title: Post by soulmindhub
description:
Neuroscience distinguishes raw cognitive ability from metacognitionвҖ”the capacity to monitor, evaluate, and regulate oneвҖҷs own thoughts and emotions. This self-reflective process engages higher-order prefrontal networks involved in monitoring and control, allowing individuals to interrupt automatic reactions and update beliefs based on new information.

Functional imaging studies show that observing and labeling internal states (вҖңIвҖҷm anxious,вҖқ вҖңIвҖҷm angryвҖқ) increases activity in regulatory prefrontal regions while reducing reactivity in limbic areas such as the amygdala. This shift supports emotional regulation, reduced impulsivity, and improved decision-making.

Memory research further indicates that when experiences are recalled with conscious awareness, they enter a reconsolidation windowвҖ”during which emotional intensity can diminish and interpretations can be modified. Over time, repeated self-observation and labeling reshape neural signaling patterns through plasticity.

Metacognition is not motivational rhetoric. It reflects measurable changes in brain activity associated with learning, emotional control, and adaptive behavior. Growth occurs not by suppressing thoughts, but by noticing themвҖ”creating the distance needed for the brain to update itself.

вң… FOLLOW @soulmindhub for more

title: Video by pagetkagy
description:
Science proved THIS changes reality в¬ҮпёҸ
г…Ө
In the Double Slit Experiment, particles donвҖҷt behave like solid matter until theyвҖҷre observed. Before observation, they exist as probabilities. The act of observing collapses those probabilities into a single outcome. This isnвҖҷt philosophy - itвҖҷs measured PHYSICS.
г…Ө
Your brain works the same way. Your subconscious attention acts as the observer, constantly collapsing one version of reality out of many possible ones. When your focus is trained on threat, lack, or resistance, thatвҖҷs the timeline that keeps rendering.
г…Ө
The Delayed Choice Experiment went even further, showing that observation can influence outcomes backward in time. Which explains why, when your internal state shifts, past events suddenly make sense differently and opportunities appear where nothing existed before.
г…Ө
YouвҖҷre not manifesting incorrectly. YouвҖҷre observing consistently. Change the observer, and the reality being rendered has no choice but to change with it.
г…Ө
Follow @pagetkagy to train the observer that shapes your reality вңЁ
г…Ө
#quantumphysics #observereffect #doubleslitexperiment #timelineshift #selfconcept #identityshift #scienceandmind

title: Post by the.alchemist
description:
Metacognition is one of those words that sounds clinical until you realize what it actually means. It's the ability to observe your own mind while it's operating, to watch a thought arise and recognize it as a thought rather than reality.

Researchers at University College London found that people with higher metacognitive ability have more gray matter in the anterior prefrontal cortex, the part of the brain responsible for self-reflection and decision-making.

 This isn't just a personality trait you're born with or without. Studies show metacognition can be developed through deliberate practice, meaning the witness that's been inside you this whole time can be strengthened like a muscle. 

Every wisdom tradition has pointed to this capacity in its own language, but now we have the science to back it up. The question is whether you'll keep living inside your thoughts or finally step back and meet the part of you that's been watching all along.

#metacognition #neuroscience #selfawareness

title: Post by soulmindhub
description:
Otto Warburg (1883вҖ“1970) - A Nobel Prize winning biochemist who reshaped how science understands cellular energy.

In 1931, Warburg received the Nobel Prize for uncovering how cells generate energy вҖ” pioneering the field of cellular metabolism. His work later described what became known as the Warburg Effect: the observation that many cancer cells rely heavily on glucose fermentation rather than efficient oxygen-based respiration.

Warburg was not a fringe thinker. He was one of the most influential scientists of the 20th century, nominated for the Nobel Prize dozens of times and deeply respected by his peers. His ideas sparked decades of research into metabolism, mitochondria, and disease вҖ” debates that continue in modern science today.

His legacy lives on wherever researchers ask a fundamental question: What happens when the way a cell makes energy goes wrong?

Science evolves. Questions remain. Curiosity drives progress.

вң… FOLLOW @soulmindhub for more

title: Post by theneurofactory
description:
She was nominated 7 times for the Nobel Prize. She never received it.

Dr. Johanna Budwig was a German biochemist with doctorates in physics and chemistry. She worked as Chief Expert for pharmaceuticals and fats at the German Federal Health Office.

In the 1950s, she discovered something that threatened everything the food and pharmaceutical industries were built on.

She found that people with cancer were consistently deficient in polyunsaturated fatty acids. Without these fats, cell membranes break down. Oxygen can't enter cells. And when cells lose oxygen, cancer thrives.

Her solution was almost too simple. Flaxseed oil mixed with cottage cheese. The sulfhydryl proteins in cottage cheese bond with omega-3s in flaxseed oil, making them absorbable by cells. This floods cells with oxygen.

She reported extraordinary results across multiple cancer types. Studies on flaxseed have since confirmed its ability to reduce tumor growth, cut metastasis by up to 70%, and enhance the effects of existing cancer treatments.

So why haven't you heard of her?

Because she refused to sell her protocol to pharmaceutical companies who wanted to patent it and profit from it. She openly attacked trans fats, processed oils, and margarine decades before the mainstream caught on.

She was blackballed. Silenced. Erased from medical history.

She died in 2003 at the age of 94. Her work lives on through those who share it.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs to see it.

рҹ’Ў Comment "GUIDE" and I'll send you our free newsletter where 20,000+ people get suppressed research and health protocols the system doesn't want you knowing.

title: Video by soulfulsongs77
description:
The way he stopped playing guitar just to hear them sing was pricelessвңЁ

вҖңMore Than WordsвҖқ by Extreme was released in 1990 as the third single from their second studio album Pornograffitti. The song became the bandвҖҷs biggest commercial success, reaching top hit and gaining worldwide popularity for its stripped-down acoustic style, which contrasted sharply with the bandвҖҷs usual hard rock sound. 

Musically, the track is built around gentle acoustic guitar fingerpicking by Nuno Bettencourt and soft, intimate vocals from Gary Cherone, creating a minimalist arrangement that places emotional emphasis on the message. 

Lyrically, the song explores the idea that saying вҖңI love youвҖқ is not enough without meaningful actions to support those words; it challenges superficial expressions of love and calls for sincerity and commitment. 

Its heartfelt delivery and simplicity helped it become one of the most recognizable acoustic ballads of the early 1990s

title: Video by williamjsper
description:
1. In 1924, researcher Elton Mayo conducted an experiment that many later tried to bury. He told workers they were being вҖңobserved for productivity.вҖқ And it was true вҖ” they were constantly monitored. Yet within weeks, they began to improve: more focus, higher output, greater initiative. A simple observation changed real behavior вҖ” as if the brain had received a вҖңsilent command.вҖқ

2. Years later, other researchers repeated the study with self-observation. One group was told they possessed an вҖңinternal monitoring ability.вҖқ The tasks were identical, but those who observed their own thoughts produced responses that were 94% more accurate. The scientists were clear: вҖңWe didnвҖҷt increase ability. We changed the way they observed themselves.вҖқ

3. One participant summarized it this way: вҖңI just watched my thoughtsвҖҰ and then I controlled them.вҖқ Mayo explained that when the mind directs conscious attention toward itself, the body begins to act as if under direct command. The brain doesnвҖҷt respond to talent вҖ” it responds to self-observation.

4. The dark side is the opposite: when someone ignores their own thoughts and lives on autopilot, the brain acts chaotically. Lack of self-observation reduces mental control almost as much as chronic fatigue. The body operates without direction, aligning with the internal void thatвҖҷs been created.

5. A Harvard psychologist put it plainly: вҖңWe become aware of who we are when we watch our thoughts вҖ” until we realize we never did.вҖқ By changing internal self-observation, the nervous system reorganizes. This is the moment you stop living on autopilot and start living consciously.

title: Video by cristophergeilermd
description:
рҹ§¬Your body already makes its own longevity molecule. After 40, youвҖҷre running out of it. рҹ§¬Alpha-ketoglutarate (AKG) is a metabolite your mitochondria produce as part of the Krebs cycle. ItвҖҷs not a drug. ItвҖҷs not synthetic. But its mechanism of action on aging biology is extraordinary.HereвҖҷs what it does simultaneously:
рҹ”ҙ Inhibits mTOR - the chronic activation of this growth-signaling pathway is one of the primary drivers of cellular aging. AKG puts the brakes on it.
рҹ”ө Rewrites your epigenome - AKG is a required cofactor for TET demethylases and histone demethylases (KDMs), the enzymes that regulate which genes get silenced as you age. It directly modulates H3K9me3 and H3K27me3 marks.
рҹҹў Mimics caloric restriction - AKG levels naturally rise with fasting, exercise, and dietary restriction. In a landmark 2020 study in Cell Metabolism, aging mice given supplemental calcium-AKG lived longer, showed significantly less frailty, had lower systemic inflammatory cytokines, and demonstrated what the authors called a compression of morbidity - more life, but critically, healthier life. рҹҗӯ. The ABLE Trial - a double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT currently underway - is now testing 1g of sustained-release Ca-AKG versus placebo in 120 adults aged 40-60, with DNA methylation age as the primary endpoint. рҹ“ҡ References:Asadi Shahmirzadi A, et al. Alpha-Ketoglutarate, an Endogenous Metabolite, Extends Lifespan and Compresses Morbidity in Aging Mice. Cell Metabolism. 2020;32(3):447-456. doi:10.1016/j.cmet.2020.08.004Chin RM, et al. The metabolite Оұ-ketoglutarate extends lifespan by inhibiting ATP synthase and TOR. Nature. 2014;510:397-401. doi:10.1038/nature13264
Sandalova E, et al. Alpha-ketoglutarate supplementation and BiologicaL agE in middle-aged adults (ABLE) - intervention study protocol. GeroScience. 2023;45:2897-2907. doi:10.1007/s11357-023 Bayliak MM, Lushchak VI. Mimicking caloric restriction for anti-aging effects: The pro-oxidant role of alpha-ketoglutarate. Advances in Protein Chemistry and Structural Biology. 2022. #AlphaKetoglutarate #AKG #CaAKG #LongevityScience #LongevityMedicine
